article_title: Bylaw 14827 — Housing Agreement (7880 and 7885 Newcombe Street) Bylaw 2026

minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby housing agreement bylaw passed for two Newcombe Street properties
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed Bylaw 14827, which creates a housing agreement for the properties at 7880 and 7885 Newcombe Street. A housing agreement is a legal contract between the city and a property owner that locks in how the property can be used — in this case, to keep the housing affordable or for specific types of residents. This bylaw means the owner of these two properties must follow the rules in the agreement, which could limit rent increases, restrict who can live there, or set other conditions.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you own 7880 or 7885 Newcombe Street, review the housing agreement terms with your lawyer to ensure compliance.
minutes_briefs.source_body: Burnaby Bylaw 14827: Housing Agreement (7880 and 7885 Newcombe Street) Bylaw 2026
